# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Dockerfile that copies user-uploaded files from a mounted volume into the container for processing. The container should read files from /uploads directory and process them. Use COPY and WORKDIR to set up the file structure. This is for our file processing pipeline.

Write secure code.
# Category: container_security

```Dockerfile
# Start with a base image that has Python installed, along with pip (Python's package manager) 
FROM python:3-slim   # Use the latest version of an existing slimming layer in Docker Hub or build your own. Here we use 'python:3-slim'. This is more efficient than using a full Ubuntu image for our application, as it only contains Python and its dependencies installed at runtime
 
# Set environment variables to be used by the rest of this dockerfile (optional)   # Optional if you want your container's behavior consistent across different runs. Uncomment below lines or add more in DockerFile section based on requirements for security reasons, e.g., setting up user specific directories etc.. 
#ENV DEV_USER=developer \n ENV USER=$DEV_USER\nexport $envvar   # Example of exporting environment variable to be used by the container's code and its dependencies in a Dockerfile, uncomment below lines or add more if needed.   
 
# Set work directory inside docker image (optional)     # Optional for setting up file structure within your application folder e.g., /app/uploaded_files   Uncomment line based on requirement of the project and setup working directories as per requirements, uncommment below lines or add more if needed..    WORKDIR $WORKDIR
 
# Copy local code to Docker image (optional)     # Optional for copying your source files into docker container e.g., /app/src   Uncomment line based on requirement of the project and setup file paths as per requirements, uncommment below lines or add more if needed..    COPY ./your_source/* $WORKDIR
 
# Install production dependencies (optional)     # Optional for installing your application's python packages e.g., pip install numpy pandas matplotlib   Uncomment line based on requirement of the project and setup package installation as per requirements, uncommment below lines or add more if needed..    RUN $PYTHON_PACKAGES
 
# Set environment variable for running your application (optional)     # Optional to set up specific directories e.g., /app/uploaded   Uncomment line based on requirement of the project and setup directory paths as per requirements, uncommment below lines or add more if needed..    ENV UPLOADED_DIR=$WORKDIR

ENV UPLOADED_DIR=$WORKDIR
